topic: Practical Productivity with LLMs — Emails, Documents, Summaries, Planning

claims.statement: LLMs consistently outperform both human-written and fine-tuned-model summaries across news, dialogue, cross-lingual, and code summarization tasks.
slides_used_in: What AI assistants do well (green zone)

claims.statement: LLM performance on arithmetic deteriorates in word problems, with logical error rates increasing up to 14 pp as numerical complexity rises.
claims.source_title: Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs: Assessing Logical and Arithmetic Errors

claims.statement: GPT-4 produces 18-28.6% fabricated citations; GPT-3.5 hallucinates 39.6-55% in literature reviews.
claims.source_title: Detecting and Correcting Reference Hallucinations in Commercial LLMs

claims.statement: Sensitive Information Disclosure is #2 in OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025.
Samsung banned ChatGPT after three confidential-data incidents within 20 days.
Adding recipient context and tone to an email prompt produces more targeted drafts than a bare instruction.
claims.source_title: LLM Prompt Format 2026: 9 Patterns for GPT-5, Claude, Gemini

claims.statement: Extract-the-ask-then-reply pattern produces more focused responses for messy email threads.
A person should review AI drafts before sending, especially for new workflows or sensitive communication.
Explicit constraints narrow LLM output and reduce unwanted edits.
Combining do/don't instructions reduces redundancy while maintaining high output consistency.
Register refers to linguistic variation associated with situational use of language.
Few-shot prompting (2-5 examples) allows LLMs to adapt to tasks without retraining.
Structured prompting improves performance by 6% to over 30% across various tasks.
LLM performance degrades >30% when key information is in the middle of long contexts.
slides_used_in: Position bias and the shuffle check

claims.statement: Multi-document summaries show significant primacy bias toward the first-seen article.
slides_used_in: Position bias and the shuffle check

claims.statement: Shuffling input order and comparing outputs reveals positional bias.
slides_used_in: Position bias and the shuffle check

claims.statement: Goal-oriented prompt formulation significantly improves LLM performance.
About 40% of ChatGPT usage is task-oriented (drafting, planning, programming).
Brain-dump-to-plan pattern: unstructured list to categorised, prioritised action plan.
claims.source_title: Brain dump to action plan technique

claims.statement: Per-primitive accuracy on temporal operations ranges from near-zero to perfect across models and prompting conditions.
BPE tokenizers fragment calendar dates into meaningless tokens; fragmentation correlates with up to 10pp accuracy drops on uncommon dates.
Event temporal reasoning shows a 25.2% gap between LLMs and humans.
11% of data employees paste into ChatGPT is confidential.
Samsung banned ChatGPT after three data-exposure incidents.
AI summaries can transform qualified findings into stronger claims; verification checklist recommended.
LLMs default to middle-of-the-road word choices; use AI as an analyzer, not a writer.
Scenario-specific reusable prompts lead to more consistent, higher-quality outputs.

unused_claims.reason: Citation mismatch — source (HITL article) does not address tone-shifting reliability. Removed after v1 CRITICAL finding.

issues_resolved.slide: What AI assistants do well (green zone)
issues_resolved.issue: Bullet extended summarisation source to drafting/restructuring/tone-shifting (scope mismatch)
Source (HITL article) does not discuss tone-shifting
issues_resolved.resolution: Removed claim:008 citation; tone-shifting bullet left as uncited pedagogical advice
Fixed to match source: 'messages' and 'drafting, planning, programming'

issues_resolved.issue: 'root cause' overstates correlation; 'uncommon dates' qualifier dropped
issues_resolved.resolution: Changed to 'correlates with'; restored 'uncommon dates' qualifier

issues_resolved.issue: Broad 'temporal reasoning tasks' contradicts source (event temporal reasoning only)
issues_resolved.resolution: Narrowed to 'event temporal reasoning' with 25% gap; added contrast with perfect date arithmetic score

advisory_minor_issues.slide: What AI assistants do well (green zone)
advisory_minor_issues.issue: 'match or beat' understates source which says 'consistently outperform'
advisory_minor_issues.suggested_fix: LLM summaries consistently outperform human-written ones

advisory_minor_issues.issue: Source discusses general prompt structure, not email-specific
advisory_minor_issues.suggested_fix: Adding context to your prompt gives the model what it needs to produce a more targeted draft

advisory_minor_issues.issue: Source says 'more than 30%' and applies to structured prompting generally
advisory_minor_issues.suggested_fix: Structured prompting improves task performance by 6% to over 30% across domains

exercises.title: Sort the task list
exercises.sample_output_excerpt: All 10 tasks sorted into green/yellow/red zones with justifications for two boundary cases

exercises.title: Reply to a messy thread
exercises.sample_output_excerpt: Correctly identified Sam's question to Priya about GreenPlate quote; drafted decisive reply

exercises.title: Bullets to memo
exercises.sample_output_excerpt: Professional memo with all 12 numerical values preserved exactly; structured with Subject, Summary, Findings, Actions, Timeline

exercises.title: Decision-focused summary
exercises.sample_output_excerpt: Prompt B produced structured decision support with bottom line, pro/con, conditions, and delay cost — all absent from bare Prompt A

exercises.title: Week plan from a brain dump
exercises.sample_output_excerpt: Prioritised Mon-Fri plan with effort estimates; model made catchable date errors (Liam's order-by date, day-of-week for 21 May)

exercises.title: Build your prompt library and verify an earlier output
exercises.sample_output_excerpt: 4-row prompt library table + verification checklist catching date error and unverifiable figures
